title: Setup ssh keys for your machines
tags: console, ssh-keygen, ssh-agent, ssh-add, keychain, auto, automatic
snippet: Automatically register SSH keysGuide to automatically add ssh private keys every time you startup your terminal.PrerequisitsFirst of all you need to generate a ssh key. There are lots of ways and encryptions to choose from. Here is an very simple example which usualy does the trick.ssh-keygen -t rsa -f ~/.ssh/&lt;name_of_key&gt;.key If you only type ssh-keygen -t rsa you will create a key named id_rsa. This key will automatically be used and this process is unnessesary.Usual steps to add SSH keys to your terminalFirst of all you need to start the ssh-agent. You usually do this by this command:eval $(ssh-agent)This should output the process id.Then you need to add the ssh key ~/.ssh/&lt;SomeKey&gt; to the ssh-agent with the following command:ssh-add &lt;path-and-filename-of-key&gt;Quick solutionThere is a different way to do this. Add your keys to keychain. With this way you dont have to use ssh-agent nor ssh-add instead you need to add the following lines to your rc file depending on which shell you are using .bashrc or .zshrc most probably. This file should be located in your home folder. To get to your home folder you can type cd or cd ~keychain &lt;keyname1&gt; &lt;keyname2&gt;. ~/.keychain/`uname -n`-shHere is a example with real names to make it easier to graspkeychain github.key gitlab.key daKey. ~/.keychain/`uname -n`-sh To make this work you need to keep your ssh keys in ~/.ssh/ folder.The last detail is to install keychain by executing the following command:sudo apt-get install keychain -yNow restart your terminal and you should see a text that tells you which ssh keys that are registered. Could look something like this: * keychain 2.8.5 ~ http://www.funtoo.org * Found existing ssh-agent: 261 * Known ssh key: /home/appx/.ssh/key1.key * Known ssh key: /home/appx/.ssh/key2.key * Known ssh key: /home/appx/.ssh/gitlab * Known ssh key: /home/appx/.ssh/bitbucket.key * Known ssh key: /home/appx/.ssh/github.key
